context.user_request: Please provide a complete, correct example of a synthetic statsd charts configuration, containing two charts – one for metric "hc.foo" and other for "hc.bar"

plan_item.summary: Task class: improve_docs — Add a second synthetic chart example to the statsd plugin example.conf so users see a concrete multi-chart configuration in action.
plan_item.edit_guidance: Append a second chart section after the existing [myexamplechart] block (before the trailing comment on line 64-65). The new section should follow the same structure: a [mysecondchart] heading with name, title, family, context, units, priority, type, and one or more dimension lines referencing a different metric pattern (e.g., myexampleapp.metric3, myexampleapp.metric4). Keep the style consistent with the existing block. After the new chart block, keep the existing closing comment 'You can add as many charts as needed'. This demonstrates that multiple charts are created by simply adding more sections within the same [app] block.
acceptance_checks: example.conf contains at least two distinct chart sections (e.g., [myexamplechart] and [mysecondchart])
Each chart section has its own name, title, family, context, units, priority, type, and at least one dimension
The second chart references different metric names than the first chart
The overall structure is valid statsd synthetic config syntax (sections within the [app] block)
No trailing comment is orphaned; 'You can add as many charts as needed' remains at end of file

context.prior_review_findings: Task adherence failure: the user requested a configuration with two charts for specific metrics 'hc.foo' and 'hc.bar'. The executor added a second chart to example.conf but used 'myexampleapp.metric3' and 'myexampleapp.metric4' instead — neither chart references the requested metrics. The existing [app] section has 'metrics = myexampleapp.*' which would not match 'hc.*' metrics at all, so this config cannot serve as a working example for the user's use case. The statsd syntax itself is valid (verified against statsd.c:1466-1518 — dimension format is 'metric_name [dim_name [type [multiplier [divisor]]]]'), and the style is consistent with the existing example. But the user's core requirement is unmet. File: src/collectors/statsd.plugin/example.conf (unstaged working tree change, lines 64-77).

review.findings: Prior review finding (wrong metrics) fully addressed. Executor created src/collectors/statsd.plugin/hc.conf with a complete, correct synthetic statsd configuration containing two charts for hc.foo and hc.bar. All config keys verified against statsd.c parser (lines 1396-1527). metrics=hc.* uses SIMPLE_PATTERN_PREFIX matching on 'hc', correctly matching hc.foo and hc.bar. Dimension type 'last' is valid (default per string2valuetype() at line 1169). Priorities 91000/91001 are unique. The new-file approach (vs editing example.conf per plan) is actually correct because example.conf has metrics=myexampleapp.* which would not match hc.* metrics. No regressions — no existing files modified.
